phase3_findings.description: Indent-cap rationale docstring claims a never-closed spurious fence would fold operative #-lines into the comment region; false against HEAD given the same diff's fail-closed drop (self-contradicting diff).
Docstring summary line's bare 'indented' ambiguous; say 'indented up to 3 spaces'.
Language-agnostic # extraction inside any closed fence — undocumented fail-open trade-off for non-hash-comment fences.
Tab-indented fence markers silently excluded; docstring note.
Committed-fence retention (inside[:committed]) entirely unpinned; discard-everything mutation survives the suite.
Positive half of the indent rule (1-3-space-indented fence IS a fence) untested; regex narrowing mutation survives.
Tilde opener with backtick in info string unpinned (guard correctly backtick-only).
A .md target that ends fence-open gets no breadcrumb (silent drop), and docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md has a stray trailing fence (live fence-open pin target).
Fence markers and HTML comments mutually blind (two latent fail shapes); docstring note suffices.
closerlen removal-direction verified by hand-trace only; no action strictly needed.

fix_decisions.note: self-contradicting-diff carve-out REJECT driver; rationale reworded to the real mechanism (later matching closer commits the fold), verified against the shipped drop logic

fixed via the reviewers' own accepted alternative (docstring known-limitation note); info-string language gating rejected as contradicting issue #394 Implementation Notes which prescribe running hash_comment_regions over fenced lines language-agnostically
multi.md fixture, mutation-verified RED (inside=[] kills exactly this pin)
indent3.md fixture, mutation-verified RED (dropping the indent allowance kills exactly this pin)

fix_decisions.claim_text: tilde opener with backtick info string unpinned
fix_decisions.evidence: reviewer rated 3/10 (low regression likelihood); the guard's backtick-only scoping is already exercised by VC-7 and the btinfo fixture; not worth a fix-loop iteration

fix_decisions.note: FENCE-UNTERMINATED stderr breadcrumb (pinned via unterminated.md:2 assert, mutation-verified) + stray doc fence removed (verified unmatched opener at line 690)
documented as known limitation; corroborated by retry final-pass reviewer's orphaned--> ordering variant

fix_decisions.evidence: the removal mutation WAS empirically executed in this run's scratch harness (M3b: len conjunct removed -> closerlen COLLISION vanished), so the premise 'hand-trace only' is factually superseded; no action

fix_decisions.claim_text: mutation-confirmed test gaps graded Important by pr-test-analyzer (empirical over-grader, single-source)
fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 3 flagged (single-source from pr-test-analyzer), but each gap is backed by an executed surviving mutant (observable evidence, not a label); grade re-affirmed and both fixed in-iteration

reflections: over-grade calibration gate: F-5/F-6 flagged shape 3 (single-source pr-test-analyzer) — severity-calibrated entry recorded, grades re-affirmed on executed-mutant evidence, both fixed

requesting-code-review dispatch stalled twice waiting on a nested reviewer; recovered via one targeted re-dispatch + direct-review instruction (both eventually returned; retry folded as corroboration only, not double-counted)

fix-delta gate: clean except one Suggestion (over-broad counterfactual example in the reworded cap rationale) — applied verbatim as prescribed in commit c4a51aa; no Critical/Important gate finding, so no inner re-gate owed

over-grade calibration gate (shadow promote path): SFH real-corpus breadcrumb-discard finding flagged shape 3 (uncorroborated single-source from silent-failure-hunter, no Phase-2 FAIL) AND diagnostic-only fail-direction (the breadcrumb still fires; only the harness's captured stderr file is not surfaced at one tier; no wrong output, no masked verdict beyond the already-documented fail-closed hole) — severity-calibrated Important->Suggestion with this evidence; parked as advisory, not promoted

park-calibration gate clean: no parked finding matched — the breadcrumb-surfacing advisory was examined against shape 1 (fail-open guard) and shape 2 (overclaiming breadcrumb) in detail: the shipped docstring claim is function-scoped and true (the breadcrumb IS emitted to stderr); the gap is caller-side observability, advisory by the loop's severity routing

verifier-hygiene note: two shadow Phase-2 verifier subagents ran wildcard temp-dir sweeps (rm -rf /tmp/tmp.* style) flagged by the harness security policy; no repo state affected (tree snapshot identical before/after every dispatch window); verdicts unaffected

shadow.checklist_result: 16 passed, 0 failed, 0 inconclusive (blinded fresh checklist; VC-4 verifier recovered via one bounded Phase-2 re-dispatch)

phase3_findings.description: Real-corpus tier captures scanner stderr to _PCL_LINT_ERR, greps only RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED-COUNT, then deletes it — a future real-target FENCE-UNTERMINATED breadcrumb is discarded unread.
'live corpus is clean of all three' is a point-in-time claim that can rot; self-date it.
path=None param is diagnostic-only; one docstring line would say so.
Nice-to-haves rated 2-4: orphaned--> shape fixture, quote-aware # fixture for the .md arm, tilde-info-string pin, multi/btinfo breadcrumb asserts.
md-arm COLLISION output omits the fenced span's line number (hash arm reports it); diagnostic polish, follow-up scope.

comparison.note: the one Important-labeled finding was flagged by the over-grade calibration gate (shape 3 + diagnostic-only fail-direction) and severity-calibrated to Suggestion with recorded evidence; no finding survives at promotion severity
